description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its length. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, with the top surface displaying a marbled pinkish-purple coloration, while the sides are solid black. The block appears solid and heavy, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. The grooved design suggests it may serve as a tactile or visual barrier, possibly for guiding or restricting movement in urban environments. The surface finish is rough and matte, indicating durability and resistance to wear. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on a marbled pinkish-purple top and solid black sides, giving it a striped, durable appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved, striped top surface with the grooves running horizontally across the block.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the grooved top surface with horizontal stripes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the solid black sides.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a tactile or visual barrier
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
